senses.gloss: to be able to do (something) (to be capable of doing)
to be able to do (something) (to be in a position to do)

etymology.text: From Middle Iranian *tāk (“arch”); compare Middle Persian [script needed] (tʾg /⁠tāg⁠/, “arch”). Ultimately from the Proto-Indo-European root *teg- (“to cover; roof”), and akin to Latin toga (“garment”) and Ancient Greek στέγος (stégos, “roof”).
